use_cases.text: Built kitchen cabinet knobs visual research gallery in <60 seconds, deployed to LAN big screen at 192.168.2.163:8766
Galley kitchens gallery 547 pins auto-pulling from rotating Pinterest queries, family-locked
STT swap to MLX-whisper trialed and reverted same-day; Apple STT canon restored, 1.5GB model purged
xen-say-worker plist patched with Aqua session keys + audio-out-guard gated on TTS sentinel — voice loop stable
Mixture-of-Agents orchestrator shipped: prompt fans to Gemini/Perplexity/NotebookLM/Grok, synthesis via OpenRouter Claude
Pin-feed widget template pushed to xlrdtech/hitthe.link/pin-feed — reusable infinite topic-swipe gallery
78 active XenBrowser tabs deduped and saved into Raindrop collection #70294184 before XenBrowser rebuild
Luckie's 3-year personal+L7S archive scraped into markdown file trees for AI agents/MCPs/CLI tools

testimonials.quote: What is that I made a web gallery of kitchen cabinet knobs in less than one minute
My cost is 10% of the average cost — I run at $104 a month, the industry average is $800 per seat
My agent is more powerful, almost like an enterprise — this is not normal agent territory
We eliminated the Google auth restriction already — that was a 365-day bottleneck. So I bought a second premium seat to keep the exponential production going
Just waking back up from manually scrapping 3 years of archived files for myself and L7S to teach the AI Agents, LLMs, Luna, MCPs, CLI terminals, automation, bots, field agents
You tag your AI agent in any chat on any platform, and it's there with full awareness and confidence
